title: Mammalian Cell Nucleus Staining
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"els5p","text":"Introductory protocol for cell nucelus staining using and imaging. This protocol uses Invitrogen™ NucBlue™ LiveReady Probes™.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"fibqh","text":"96 well plate with pre-cultured cells","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"36fuf","text":"Aluminum foil","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"et6vl","text":"NucBlue™ LiveReady Probes™ Reagent solution","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bnolk","text":"DPBS","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Using micropipette, remove cell culture media from each well.
Wash each well twice with 100 µL DPBS.
Add 100 µL NucBlue™ solution to each well.
Cover with aluminum foil. The aluminum foil is simply to prevent photobleaching of the fluorescent dye.
creator: Kenneth Schackart
